reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome, real download+run, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — real transformers.js@3.7.5 run in a Web Worker: fresh profile → Download (~14 MB) → auto-init ready → Classify on the cats sample returned tabby 71.1%, tiger cat 25.9%, Egyptian cat 2.5% (correct ImageNet labels), 40 ms WASM, 1001 classes. Pre-verified in Node: mobilenet_v2_1.0_224 fp32 on the HF tiger.jpg → tiger 0.70 via both pipeline and low-level processor→model. conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. screenshots: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/w17bshots/mobilenet-classification-{light,dark,mobile}.png
rubric.notes: 40 ms vs ConvNeXt's 226 ms and ViT's heavier run on the same device — the mobile-backbone latency story, measured.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — output.logits is [1,1001] (ImageNet-1k + a background class at index 0, the TF-Slim convention); worker computes softmax + top-k + entropy + margin. Semantic sanity holds: cats photo ranks cat classes top; See-inside shows logit next to softmax %; the size/latency table fills its 'latency here' cell with the real measured 40 ms. conformance: io-shape-semantic=manual (agent verdict recorded).
rubric.notes: 1001 outputs — one more than the ViT/ConvNeXt demos (1000); surfaced explicitly on the page.

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): declares-model-id=pass (onnx-community/mobilenet_v2_1.0_224 linked to card), declares-task=pass (image-classification), declares-backend=pass (WebAssembly), declares-quantisation=pass (fp32), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker in the shared ClassifierEngine). The at-a-glance + a <details> honestly explain WHY fp32 not q8 (MobileNet's depthwise convs quantise degenerately — ConvInteger has no WASM kernel and the uint8/q8 builds emit garbage; verified).
rubric.notes: dtype fp32 recorded honestly in models.json + conformance; the substitute-model note is explicit (onnx-community, not a mislabel of a different family).

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — fresh profile shows 'Download model (~14 MB)'; clicking streams progress and the shared loader auto-inits to 'Model ready'. Cached reload → state=ready with NO Download button (only 'Clear cached model'); all four sub-pages auto-init from the session cache to ready with 0 console errors. conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass, auto-init-on-cached=manual.
rubric.notes: Verified across the state matrix: first visit + current-cached + sub-page reuse.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — streamed download progress; Retry surfaced by the shared loader; the webcam demo has an honest 'No camera available — looping a sample image instead' fallback when getUserMedia is denied/absent; the cascade demo streams a ViT ~88 MB download line only when it escalates. Runs on the WASM path with no GPU. conformance: live-status-region=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass; honest-progress-retry-offline + runs-without-webgpu-fallback = manual.
rubric.notes: getUserMedia degradation is explicit and labelled, never a blank panel.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — Classify button + top-k slider; keyboard-operable dropzone + sample thumbnails; the webcam Start/Stop; the wild downscale + JPEG-quality sliders (live px readout); the cascade escalation-threshold slider + run button — all operable. conformance: controls-operable=manual.

Agent verdict — every label comes from the on-device worker run (verified real output tabby 71.1% / tiger cat 25.9%). The webcam overlay, degradation test, and MobileNet→ViT cascade all reuse the real ClassifierEngine; no canned result anywhere. conformance: no-fake-output=manual.

conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. role=status aria-live loader + statuses; probability bars are role=meter with aria-valuenow/aria-label; sample thumbnails keyboard-operable; the <video> has aria-label; sliders have wrapping labels with live <span> readouts.

Agent verdict — desktop 1280×800 (light) and mobile 360×740 DPR3 both render with no horizontal overflow; the at-a-glance + size/latency tables wrap (long model id wraps within its cell); the webcam video is inline-size:100% max-block-size:360px; the cascade/ensemble versus-grid stacks on mobile. responsive-check programmatic pass on both classes; screenshots reviewed. conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass, parity-both-classes=manual.

Agent verdict — all inference runs in a Web Worker; the webcam loop is requestAnimationFrame-paced, single-flighted (a busy flag prevents overlapping inferences), and pauses on document.hidden; getUserMedia tracks are stopped on Stop. Real per-frame latency ~40 ms keeps the live feed fluid. 0 console errors. conformance: inp-stays-low=manual.
rubric.notes: The mobile backbone is the whole reason the live webcam loop is feasible.

guidanceConsulted.query: real-time webcam getUserMedia canvas loop without blocking main thread, pause when hidden
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Drive the live loop with requestAnimationFrame (auto-throttled when hidden), guard on document.hidden, and stop media tracks when finished.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the practical webcam loop is rAF-paced, single-flighted with a busy flag so it never runs two inferences at once, pauses on visibilitychange (document.hidden), and stops all getUserMedia tracks on Stop; inference itself is in a Web Worker.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0 console errors; live overlay updates at ~40 ms/frame; honest no-camera fallback loops a sample

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel + comparison layout without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Flexbox/grid with intrinsic sizing + flex-wrap; repeat(auto-fit, minmax()) for cards that stack on narrow screens; min-inline-size:0 / overflow-wrap for long unbreakable content.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — run panel uses flex:1 1 280px + flex-wrap; the cascade/ensemble use .versus-grid grid repeat(auto-fit, minmax(240px,1fr)); the at-a-glance and size/latency tables wrap; the long onnx-community model id wraps within its cell (verified on mobile).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; screenshots w17bshots/mobilenet-classification-{light,mobile}.png show no horizontal overflow

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live status region, labelled sliders, keyboard controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label every control; announce state in a role=status aria-live region; keep custom controls keyboard-operable; adequate tap targets.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live loader + statuses, labelled sliders with live <span> readouts, keyboard-operable dropzone + thumbnails, aria-label on the <video>, chips/buttons ≥40px.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page nav.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css; the page links it.

openQuestions: MobileNetV2 q8/uint8 are degenerate in WASM (ConvInteger unimplemented + garbage labels) — is there a per-channel int8 export or a MobileNetV3/V4 build whose quantised weights actually run correctly to shrink the download further?
Could WebNN or a WebGPU execution provider push the per-frame webcam latency well below the current WASM ~40 ms on capable devices?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a three-way MobileNet-vs-ConvNeXt-vs-ViT latency+accuracy benchmark on the same image set, making the size/speed/accuracy trade-off across the backbones directly comparable.
Investigate a correctly-quantised MobileNet ONNX (per-channel int8 or a v3/v4 build) that runs in WASM, to cut the ~14 MB fp32 download.

summary: Revision-1 agent build+matrix pass for mobilenet-classification: a NEW image-classification demo for a DISTINCT efficient mobile backbone (MobileNetV2, ~3.5M params) on the same ImageNet task as the built ViT/ConvNeXt. Real ~14 MB download + on-device transformers.js fp32 run verified in headless Chrome (cats sample → tabby 71.1%, tiger cat 25.9%, 40 ms WASM, 1001 classes incl. the background class). The 'tiny + fast' story is measured (40 ms vs ConvNeXt 226 ms) and the size/latency table makes it concrete. Ladder: real-time webcam tagging (the mobile use case, rAF-paced worker loop + honest no-camera fallback), a degradation torture test, and a MobileNet-prefilter → ViT-confirm cascade. dtype fp32 is recorded + explained honestly (q8 degenerate for this model — verified). Cached reload auto-inits with no Download button; all 4 sub-pages auto-init clean; light+dark and mobile+desktop reviewed. 23 immutable conformance assertions authored; both gates green.
